LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/RESPONDENT AZITA HOUSHANGI'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DENYING REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL AND VACATING DISMISSAL ENTERED SEPTEMBER 21, 2017 IS GRANTED. THE COURT RE-AFFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING ISSUED ON SEPTEMBER 27, 2017. COURT ORDERS COUNSEL TO MEET, CONFER AND AGREE ON A NEW TRIAL DATE. COUNSEL FOR MS. HOUSHANGI IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER TRACKING THE COURT'S RULING, COMPLYING WITH CRC RULE 3.1312 AND RESTORING CASE TO THE COURT TRIAL CALENDAR IN DEPARTMENT 206 ON THE DATE AGREED TO BY COUNSEL. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2017 Vacated

Plaintiff/Respondent'S Motion For An Order Denying Request For Dismissal And Vacating Dismissal
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Adrian G. Driscoll of the law offices of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (415-788-1900) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Theresa J. Bichsel of the California Department of Industrial Relations, Labor Commissioner's Office (415-486-2083) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

Plaintiff/Respondent Azita Houshangi'S Motion For An Order Denying Request For Dismissal And Vacating Dismissal entered September 21, 2017 is GRANTED. The court re-affirms the tentative ruling issued on September 27, 2017. Court orders counsel to meet, confer and agree on a new trial date. Counsel for Ms. Houshangi is required to prepare a proposed order tracking the court's ruling, complying with CRC Rule 3.1312 and restoring case to the court trial calendar in department 206 on the date agreed to by counsel. Per Miller v. Foremost Motors, Inc. (1993) 16 Cal App. 4th 1271, which remains good law after the 2000 amendment to Labor Code 98.2, defendant/appellant N&A Medical Supply, Inc. may not file a dismissal of this case without the consent of Ms. Houshangi. The court does not address the effect of N&A's desire no longer to pursue its appeal on any award of attorney's fees in this case. That issue will need to be decided if and when a motion for fees is filed. Nothing in either the language of the 2000 amendment or the legislative history of that amendment which is available at www.leginfo.ca.gov states or suggests that the 2000 amendment was intended to legislatively overrule Miller.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR No. 6840, e-mail: mlyon@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Plaintiff/Respondent'S Motion For An Order Denying Request For Dismissal And Vacating Dismissal
Off Calendar Sep-28-2017 Continued To Oct-04-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court sets aside the tentative ruling and continues the matter.

Plaintiff/Respondent'S Motion For An Order Denying Request For Dismissal And Vacating Dismissal entered on September 21, 2017 is CONTINUED to October 4, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 to be heard by Judge Kahn.

The tentative ruling for that date shall be as follows:

GRANTED. The case is restored to the October 10, 2017 court trial calendar in department 206. Per Miller v. Foremost Motors, Inc. (1993) 16 Cal App. 4th 1271, which remains good law after the 2000 amendment to Labor Code 98.2, defendant/appellant N&A Medical Supply, Inc. may not file a dismissal of this case without the consent of Ms. Houshangi. The court does not address the effect of N&A's desire no longer to pursue its appeal on any award of attorney's fees in this case. That issue will need to be decided if and when a motion for fees is filed. Nothing in either the language of the 2000 amendment or the legislative history of that amendment which is available at www.leginfo.ca.gov states or suggests that the 2000 amendment was intended to legislatively overrule Miller.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (L&M302)
